metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your little one in Hackney and beyond! From candlelit concerts for babies to exploring the natural world at the Natural History Museum, there's something for every 3-year-old to enjoy. Don't miss the chance to visit city farms, explore the Science Museum, or enjoy a family-friendly walk in one of London's many parks.

activities.title: Candlelight Concert for Babies
activities.description: Gentle classical music in a softly lit, candle-style setting, designed to be calm and sensory-friendly for tiny listeners
activities.whyItWorks: Calm and sensory-friendly, perfect for very young children. Relaxing atmosphere and gentle music.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and overground to Liverpool Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check times and book in advance.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed for children, with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions. Explore the Mini Museum for under 2s, and spaces designed to spark imagination and play.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers sensory experiences, interactive exhibits, and a dedicated space for toddlers. Encourages imagination and creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A community farm with a variety of animals, gardens, and opportunities to learn about farming and nature. Check out their amazing line up of Easter activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and a chance to connect with nature. Perfect for a short, engaging visit.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for special events and feeding times.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds, plus a Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling, crafts and singalong sessions for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and imaginative play areas designed specifically for young children. Offers a range of activities to stimulate creativity and engagement.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for popular events.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Get up close with farmyard animals in the heart of Hackney. A great place to see chickens, pigs, goats and more.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to see farm animals without leaving the city! Good for a short, free visit.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney! Check local bus routes or walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for opening hours and events.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme, perfect for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for toddlers, offering a safe and engaging play environment. Part of the larger Olympic Park, offering plenty of space to run around.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk into the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Westfield shopping center for lunch or snacks.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.description: One of the largest city farms, with free entry and plenty of animals. Check out their Easter activities for some affordable fun this Easter.
activities.whyItWorks: Large farm with a variety of animals and open space. A great option for a free day out with plenty to see and do.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Mudchute station.
transport.tip: Check their website for special events and feeding times.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: Join their annual Easter Egg Hunt and enjoy a delicious chocolate egg at the café! There will be other great activities running during your visit so check them out.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and special Easter activities. A fun and engaging experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and tube to Vauxhall, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for specific dates and times of Easter events.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating creatures, and the wonders of the natural world. Family-friendly activities and exhibits throughout the museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Huge dinosaur exhibit, interactive displays, and free entry. A classic London museum that appeals to all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit on a weekday to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works through interactive exhibits and displays. The Garden is an interactive space for younger children to explore construction, water, light and sound through play. The Pattern Pod is great for light exploration, sensory and interactive fun.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, dedicated play areas for young children, and free entry. A fun and educational experience for the whole family.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book free tickets online in advance to avoid queues. Visit the Wonderlab for hands-on experiments (paid).

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: A stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city. A great way to get a scenic view of London and the greenery and plants make it a sensory experience.
activities.whyItWorks: Scenic views, greenery, and a unique experience. Great for a sensory experience and photo opportunities.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and tube to Monument station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, even though it's free.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Scenic park with gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo (Battersea Children's Zoo, paid entry). Pagoda views, picnic areas, child-friendly activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Large park with a variety of activities for children. Great for a full day out with plenty to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and tube to Sloane Square, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for events and activities.

activities.title: Kew Gardens
activities.description: Discover stunning gardens and flower displays. Don't miss seasonal events. Treetop Walkway, children's garden and family attractions. During the Easter Holidays 2026: Octonauts Above & Beyond. This Easter, join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission.
activities.whyItWorks: Large outdoor space, beautiful gardens, and activities for children. A great option for a day out in nature.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Richmond, then a short walk or bus ride.
transport.tip: Check the website for seasonal events and activities.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover the history of London's waterfront through interactive exhibits and displays. Mudlarks is a soft play area designed for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, child-friendly displays, and a soft play area. A fun and educational experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Canary Wharf, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and activities.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate ship wooden playground, sensory trail, teepees and plenty of seating for parents.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a themed playground with a pirate ship, sensory trails, and teepees. Provides a fun and engaging outdoor experience for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or Queensway, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: A unique playground and park for children. It is fully enclosed and has a variety of play equipment, a sandpit, and a small farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and enclosed play area with a variety of activities. Provides a great option for a free day out with plenty to keep children entertained.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Russell Square or Holborn, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry when accompanying a child.

activities.title: Easter Family Activities at Tower Bridge – Moving Pictures
activities.dates: Tuesday 31 March, Tuesday 7 April and Saturday 11 April 2026
activities.description: Interactive drop-in activity inspired by phenakistoscopes – popular 19th-century toys that demonstrated one of the earliest forms of animation. Discover how the illusion of motion works before creating your own spinning animation featuring another Victorian icon: Tower Bridge itself.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activity, educational, and suitable for all ages. Combines making, movement and mechanical marvels.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the bus to Tower Bridge.
transport.tip: No booking required, drop-in throughout the day.

activities.title: Spring into Wild Fangs at Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Workshops on the life cycles of various creatures, featuring the Wild Fangs team showcasing animals and their development. Attendees can engage with some animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, interactive workshops, and family-friendly activities. Combines learning with fun.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.price: Included with entry to the gardens
activities.description: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive, outdoor, and educational. Perfect for young explorers who love the Octonauts.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Richmond, then a short walk or bus ride.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific dates and times.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor, interactive, and rewarding. A classic Easter activity that's fun for the whole family.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court from Waterloo, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekends and holidays.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Ham House and Garden
activities.price: £3.50 per child, plus normal National Trust admission
activities.description: Enjoy fun activities, game stations and an exciting egg trail through this glorious garden in Richmond. Inspired by the British Civil Wars, the trail introduces children to historic themes through interactive tasks and activities along the route.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor, interactive, and educational. A unique Easter activity set in a historic location.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Richmond, then a bus or walk to Ham House.
transport.tip: Check the National Trust website for specific dates and times.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Each egg reveals a secret letter and a riddle to solve and at the end there's a chocolate prize!
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and a fun Easter egg hunt. A classic combination for a great day out.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekends and holidays.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Fulham Palace
activities.description: The self-guided trail weaves through both the historic palace interiors and the surrounding gardens, inviting children to explore a site that has welcomed visitors for centuries.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor, interactive and educational. Set within one of London's oldest continually inhabited buildings.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Putney Bridge and then walk or bus
transport.tip: No advance booking required

activities.title: Royal Mews Easter Egg Expedition
activities.description: Families can follow an Easter trail around the working stables at Buckingham Palace. Look out for clues as you explore the historic carriages and courtyard.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun day out to enjoy nature and have fun.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Victoria and then walk
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance
